fab_section_md:
- King Jaehaerys remade the realm's roads—roseroad, ocean road, river road, goldroad—filling ruts and bridging streams for decades. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:15)
- His monument at Oldtown reads that he unified the kingdoms. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:15)
- He appointed Martyn Tyrell as his new master of coin. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:15)
- He sentenced Ser Lucamore to be gelded and sent to the Wall for breaking his vows. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:91)
- He sent Vaegon to the Citadel at Oldtown. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:167)
identity_line: Jaehaerys — King Jaehaerys remade the realm's roads—roseroad, ocean road, river road, goldroad—filling ruts and bridging streams for decades.

fab_section_md:
- Queen Alysanne bore Jaehaerys many children across these years and grieved deeply at each infant loss. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:17)
- She took great pride in arranging marriages but struggled bitterly to match her four younger children. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:101)
- She dealt with the three families of Ser Lucamore, decreeing his children bear bastard names. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:95)
identity_line: Alysanne — Queen Alysanne bore Jaehaerys many children across these years and grieved deeply at each infant loss.

fab_section_md:
- Baelon the Brave, the Spring Prince, followed Aemon into knighthood in 73 AC, jousting at Old Oak as a mystery knight called the Silver Fool. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:51)
- He claimed the great she-dragon Vhagar, unridden since Visenya's death twenty-nine years before. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:55)
- In 75 AC he wed his sister Alyssa; in 77 AC she bore him a son, Viserys. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:171)
identity_line: Baelon the Brave, the Spring Prince, followed Aemon into knighthood in 73 AC, jousting at Old Oak as a mystery knight called the Silver Fool.

fab_section_md:
- Princess Rhaenys, daughter of Aemon and Jocelyn, was born on the seventh day of the seventh moon of 74 AC. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:145)
- Many hailed her as next in line for the Iron Throne after her father, and Alysanne called her "our queen to be." (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:147)
identity_line: Rhaenys — Princess Rhaenys, daughter of Aemon and Jocelyn, was born on the seventh day of the seventh moon of 74 AC.

fab_section_md: - Corlys Velaryon, grandson to the Lord of the Tides and the future Sea Snake, was already a celebrated mariner when Daella was sent to meet him at thirteen. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:205)
identity_line: Corlys Velaryon, grandson to the Lord of the Tides and the future Sea Snake, was already a celebrated mariner when Daella was sent to meet him at thirteen.

fab_section_md: - Prince Gaemon, the eleventh child, was born 73 AC and named for Gaemon the Glorious; he died an infant. (fab-the-long-reign-cont-14-p02:65)
identity_line: Gaemon — Prince Gaemon, the eleventh child, was born 73 AC and named for Gaemon the Glorious; he died an infant.
